articles.title: Lakers Lose Momentum as Rockets Snap Series Lead in Game 5
articles.original_title: The Lakers have lost all momentum against the Rockets
articles.summary: Entering Game 5, Los Angeles held a comfortable series edge over Houston, but the Rockets rallied at Crypto.com Arena to win the contest and halt the Lakers' push toward a sweep. The loss shifted the series momentum and left the Lakers scrambling to regain control.
key_points: Houston won Game 5 on the road, preventing a Lakers sweep.
The victory halted the Lakers' series momentum after three straight wins.
Los Angeles now faces a must‑win situation to avoid falling behind 3‑2.

articles.title: Three Takeaways from the Lakers’ Game 5 Defeat to the Rockets
articles.original_title: 3 takeaways from Lakers’ Game 5 loss vs. Rockets
articles.summary: Former player‑turned‑analyst JJ Redick emphasized the difficulty of closing out a playoff series after the Rockets survived a Game 4 elimination bid. In Game 5, the Lakers failed to finish the series at home, exposing lapses on both ends of the floor.
key_points: The Rockets showed resilience by winning a critical Game 5 at Crypto.com Arena.
Lakers struggled to execute in the clutch, missing key shots and defensive stops.
Redick highlighted the need for the Lakers to “kill” opponents decisively in future games.

articles.title: Rockets Edge Lakers 99‑93 in Game 5 – Yahoo Sports Recap
articles.original_title: Houston Rockets 99 - Los Angeles Lakers 93: Final score, results, recap, box score, stats - Yahoo Sports
articles.summary: Houston defeated Los Angeles 99‑93 in Game 5 of the first‑round series, handing the Lakers their first loss of the matchup. The box score reflected a balanced Rockets effort and a sub‑par offensive output from the Lakers.
key_points: Final score: Rockets 99, Lakers 93.
Lakers shot below their series average, struggling to generate offense.
Houston’s defense limited LeBron James and Anthony Davis, contributing to the win.

key_points: Play‑by‑play logs show Houston taking a late‑game lead and holding it.

The live feed captured the Rockets’ defensive adjustments that stifled Los Angeles.

articles.title: Sports Illustrated: 3 Takeaways From Lakers' Disappointing Game 5 Loss vs. Rockets
articles.original_title: 3 Takeaways From Lakers' Disappointing Game 5 Loss vs. Rockets - Sports Illustrated
articles.summary: Sports Illustrated broke down the Lakers’ 99‑93 defeat, pointing to offensive inefficiency, defensive lapses, and the inability to close out the series at home. The analysis calls for immediate adjustments if Los Angeles hopes to stay alive.
key_points: Lakers shot poorly and failed to capitalize on second‑chance opportunities.
Defensive rotations broke down, allowing Houston to score in transition.
The loss forces the Lakers into a do‑or‑die situation in Game 6.

articles.summary: LeBron James didn't get overly excited last week when his Los Angeles Lakers rolled to a shocking 3-0 series lead over the Houston Rockets despite playing without their top two scorers.
